translated: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words. And they journeyed eastward and found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there. They said to one another, "Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly." And they had bricks for stone and bitumen for mortar. Then they said, "Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower whose top reaches the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered over the face of the whole earth." But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower that the people were building. And the Lord said, "Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do. Nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them. Come, let us go down and confuse their language there, so that they may not understand one another's speech." So the Lord scattered them from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city. Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth, and from there the Lord scattered them over the face of all the earth."

text: Bo̱ fo̱ kanye‑o, Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ a ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ nkyu nse̱na, na Yeesuu mɔ wo̱re̱ bo̱ bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.
translated: Come here, Jesus healed the sick and washed with water, and Jesus commanded them to go into the house of the one who was called.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: Now King Herod had died. But when he died, the Angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, "Return to the land of Egypt."

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Yooo, mo̱ a nu. Mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kya mò̱.”
translated: Jesus said, "Here you are. You will go prepare your place."

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a baa agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ na asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye na mmu na bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.
translated: Bamo was sent to deliver a message but did not go. Bamo took the book and went to the riverbank, to the meeting place, to the people, and he gave thanks to Eworese, Eworese, who had guided him there. That is Bamo's story.

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱fɔ mò̱ kuri mò̱ nyare̱‑ana e̱ kye̱na ako̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi.
translated: Why do you call me good? No one is holy except God alone. Do you keep the commandments?

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ kanye‑o, Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ a ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ nkyu nse̱na, na Yeesuu mɔ wo̱re̱ bo̱ bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.
translated: When you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, and Jesus will reward you openly.
